title: Sharing Makes More Fun
lesson: Sharing your toys makes playtime more fun for everyone.
prompts: In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits happily playing with a pile of colourful building blocks, with a cheerful smile. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Warm sunlight. Exactly one child in the frame, and no other people.

In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, holding a colourful toy block a little close, with an uncertain, protective expression. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} walks up with open hands and a hopeful, friendly smile, wanting to join the play. Make the interaction read physically: the girl's open hands and gaze reach toward the colourful block the child holds, while the child curls that block close. Warm sunlight. Exactly two children in the frame, and no other people.

In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, with a kind, gentle smile, holding out a colourful building block to offer it. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} reaches forward to accept the block with a delighted expression. Make the interaction read physically: the child holds the colourful block out and the girl's reaching hands and gaze meet that same block between them. Warm sunlight. Exactly two children in the frame, and no other people.

In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, with a happy smile, stacking colourful blocks together with a friend. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits beside the growing block tower, placing a block and grinning. Make the interaction read physically: the child's and the girl's hands both set blocks onto the one growing block tower between them, their gazes on the tower. Warm sunlight. Exactly two children in the frame, and no other people.

In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, laughing joyfully and pointing up at a tall, colourful tower they built together. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} laughs and claps with delight at the finished tower. Make the interaction read physically: the child's pointing hand and the girl's gaze both aim up at the tall finished tower between them. Warm sunlight. Exactly two children in the frame, and no other people.

In a sunny backyard garden, on a picnic blanket at golden hour, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits, with a warm, contented smile, sharing a small bowl of fruit snacks, the tall colourful block tower they built earlier still standing on the blanket just beside them. {INPUT_IMAGE_IDENTITY} Nearby, {GENDER_F_AGE_06} sits close, reaching into the bowl for a fruit snack and smiling happily, the two friends resting together after playing. Make the interaction read physically: the girl reaches into the small bowl of fruit snacks the child holds, both gazes on that bowl. Soft warm evening light. Exactly two children in the frame, and no other people.
gists: In a sunny backyard garden on a picnic blanket, the child sits alone happily building with a pile of colourful blocks.
On the garden blanket, the child holds a block a little tightly while another young girl walks up with open hands, hoping to join — the child is hesitant to share.
On the garden blanket, the child holds out a block to offer it while the girl reaches to accept it, delighted — the child decides to share.
On the garden blanket, the child and the girl stack colourful blocks together into a growing tower, both happy.
On the garden blanket, the child laughs and points up at the tall finished tower while the girl claps with delight.
On the garden blanket at golden hour, the child and the girl rest together sharing a bowl of fruit snacks, the tall block tower still standing beside them — contented friends after playing.
texts: {NAME} sat in the sunny garden with a big pile of colourful blocks. "Building is so much fun!" said {NAME}, stacking them up high.
A friend came over and watched. "Can I play too?" she asked. {NAME} held one block tight. "Hmm..." said {NAME}, not quite sure.
Then {NAME} thought for a moment and held a block out. "Here — you can play with me!" said {NAME}. "Yes, please!" said the friend, beaming.
Together they stacked the blocks higher and higher. "Up it goes!" laughed {NAME}. "Look at our tower grow!" said the friend. Sharing made it better.
"We did it!" they both cheered. The tower was taller than ever! {NAME} pointed up high and laughed and laughed.
At the end, they sat side by side and shared a yummy snack. "Best day ever," said {NAME}. Playing together was the very most fun of all.
